retry_reason: The put operation is unconditional (if root is non-NULL) and refcount_dec_and_test is safe; if the refcount reaches zero the object is freed, so retrying would be a double-free.
contract_summary: btrfs_put_root is a put-wrapper that releases a reference on a btrfs_root. When the last reference is dropped (refcount becomes zero), it frees all internal resources (inodes xarray, delayed_nodes xarray, anon_dev, extent buffers) and kfrees the root itself. After calling this function, the caller must not access the root pointer. The function also removes the root from a debug leak list (only in DEBUG builds). This function represents the release lifecycle stage of the btrfs_root object.

judge.reason: The function's primary purpose is to decrement the reference count of a btrfs_root and, when the count reaches zero, free the object and associated resources. The cleanup logic is inherent to the put operation and minimal.

check.reason: refcount_dec_and_test(&root->refs) is called on the first parameter root. The access path root.refs.refs correctly traces from parameter root to the atomic_t field: root->refs (refcount_t) then its internal refs (atomic_t). The type chain btrfs_root--refcount_t--atomic_t matches the actual struct definitions. The operation is a put (decrement and test). Segment counts (3 each) are consistent.
